story.soThat: development ve testing için sample data oluşturabileyim
story.tasks: - Task 1: Create seed.ts file structure ve setup (AC: 1) - Subtask 1.1: prisma/seed.ts dosyası oluştur - Subtask 1.2: PrismaClient import ve initialize et - Subtask 1.3: bcrypt ve uuid dependencies import et - Subtask 1.4: Main async seed() function tanımla - Subtask 1.5: Prisma connect/disconnect logic ekle - Subtask 1.6: Error handling ve try-catch wrapper ekle - Task 2: Implement sample domain creation (AC: 2) - Subtask 2.1: Domain entity için sabit UUID generate et - Subtask 2.2: Upsert pattern ile domain oluştur - Subtask 2.3: Domain ID'yi variable'da sakla - Subtask 2.4: Console log ekle - Task 3: Implement core permissions seeding (AC: 2) - Subtask 3.1: USERS module permissions array tanımla (CREATE, VIEW, UPDATE, DELETE) - Subtask 3.2: Loop ile her permission için upsert yap - Subtask 3.3: Created permission count track et - Subtask 3.4: Console log ekle - Task 4: Implement admin user seeding (AC: 2) - Subtask 4.1: Admin password'u bcrypt ile hash'le - Subtask 4.2: Admin user object oluştur - Subtask 4.3: Upsert admin user - Subtask 4.4: Console log ekle - Task 5: Implement test user seeding (AC: 2) - Subtask 5.1: Test password'u bcrypt ile hash'le - Subtask 5.2: Test user object oluştur - Subtask 5.3: Upsert test user - Subtask 5.4: Console log ekle - Task 6: Add package.json seed command (AC: 3) - Subtask 6.1: package.json dosyasını aç - Subtask 6.2: scripts section'ına prisma:seed ekle - Subtask 6.3: Alternatif prisma.seed key'i ekle - Subtask 6.4: Test npm run prisma:seed - Task 7: Implement idempotency ve final success message (AC: 4, 5) - Subtask 7.1: Tüm entity creation'larda upsert pattern kullan - Subtask 7.2: Seed script'i iki kez çalıştır ve test et - Subtask 7.3: Final success message oluştur - Subtask 7.4: Error case message ekle - Task 8: Integration testing ve validation (AC: All) - Subtask 8.1: Fresh database ile seed test et - Subtask 8.2: Query admin user verify - Subtask 8.3: Query test user verify - Subtask 8.4: Query permissions verify - Subtask 8.5: Seed'i tekrar çalıştır test - Subtask 8.6: Login test simulation

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. AC-1.6.1: prisma/seed.ts oluşturulmuş (async function seed()) 2. AC-1.6.2: Seed script creates: - Admin user (admin@boilerplate.com, password: Admin123!, bcrypt hashed) - Test user (user@boilerplate.com, password: User123!, bcrypt hashed) - Core permissions (USERS.CREATE, USERS.VIEW, USERS.UPDATE, USERS.DELETE) - Sample domain (domainID: uuid(), name: "Default Domain") 3. AC-1.6.3: package.json'a seed command eklendi: "prisma:seed": "ts-node prisma/seed.ts" 4. AC-1.6.4: Seed script idempotent (upsert pattern, duplicate hata vermiyor) 5. AC-1.6.5: Console'a success message: "✅ Seed completed: X users, Y permissions"

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Complete acceptance criteria for seed data script. Admin/test users with bcrypt hashed passwords, core USERS permissions, idempotent upsert pattern.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: User entity schema: id (UUID), domainID (UUID), email (unique), passwordHash, firstName, lastName, emailVerified, isActive. Permission entity: id, module, action (unique [module, action]), description.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Password hashing: bcrypt with 10+ rounds. No secrets in version control. Multi-tenancy: domainID field on all tables.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Seed idempotency: upsert pattern (check if exists before insert). Email uniqueness prevents duplicate users.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Seed data for development: admin user, test user, core permissions (USERS.CREATE/VIEW/UPDATE/DELETE), sample domain. Dependencies: Story 1.5 (migration system).

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: Password hashing best practices: bcrypt algorithm, minimum 10 rounds, never store plaintext passwords.

doc.title: Architecture Document
doc.snippet: domainID pattern: UUID field on all entities, queries must filter by domainID, indexes on domainID for performance.

doc.title: Story 1.5 - Migration System
doc.snippet: Init migration created all 11 core tables. Database empty and ready for seed. Prisma v6.18.0. User table: id, domainID, email (unique), passwordHash, emailVerified, isActive. Permission table: module, action (unique constraint).

artifact.reason: PrismaClient wrapper - seed script will use this service to interact with database
Entity definitions for users and permissions that seed script will populate
SQL DDL for tables that seed will populate - shows exact column structure and constraints

npm.package: Prisma ORM client for database operations

story-context.constraints: - Seed script location: prisma/seed.ts (standard Prisma convention) - Idempotency required: use upsert pattern (where clause for uniqueness) - Password hashing: bcrypt with 10 rounds minimum - Domain ID: Predictable UUID for consistency ("default-domain-123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000" pattern) - Multi-tenancy: domainID field required on User entities - Email uniqueness: Enforced by database constraint, handle in upsert - Permissions format: Module.Action (e.g., "USERS.CREATE") - Unique constraint: [module, action] on permissions table - Console output: Success/failure messages for developer feedback - Error handling: try-catch with process.exit(1) on failure - Database connection: Connect before operations, disconnect in finally block - No hardcoded secrets: Passwords are for development only (documented in code comments)

interface.name: bcrypt.hash()
interface.signature: async bcrypt.hash(data: string, saltOrRounds: number): Promise<string>

tests.standards: Story 1.6 testing follows integration test pattern: actual database operations with fresh database state. Use Prisma queries to verify seed data after execution. Test idempotency by running seed twice and asserting no duplicates or errors.
tests.locations: - prisma/seed.ts (inline validation with console logs) - Integration tests: test/ or __tests__/ directory (future - Epic 9) - Manual testing: npm run prisma:seed execution and database queries
tests.ideas:
**AC-1.6.1 Test:** File existence check (prisma/seed.ts exists, exports async seed function)
**AC-1.6.2 Test:** - Query users table: COUNT(*) = 2 - Verify admin@boilerplate.com exists with correct fields - Verify user@boilerplate.com exists with correct fields - Query permissions table: COUNT(*) = 4 - Verify USERS.CREATE, USERS.VIEW, USERS.UPDATE, USERS.DELETE exist - Query for domain: name = "Default Domain"
**AC-1.6.3 Test:** package.json contains "prisma:seed" script, npm run prisma:seed executes successfully
**AC-1.6.4 Test:** - Fresh database → run seed → verify data - Run seed AGAIN → no errors, same data count (no duplicates) - Check email uniqueness not violated
**AC-1.6.5 Test:** - Capture console output during seed - Assert contains "✅ Seed completed" - Assert mentions user count and permission count
**Password Hash Validation Test:** - Extract passwordHash from admin user - Use bcrypt.compare("Admin123!", hash) → should return true - Same for test user with "User123!"
